claims.text: Only about 50% of web traffic is human, and that share is shrinking every year.

evidence.quote:
actually it's more than
you think studies say that only about 50
percent of web traffic is human and that
number is going down every year in 2013
the times reported that half of y

claims.rationale: Imperva / Distil / Cloudflare bad-bot reports routinely show humans around 50-60% of total web traffic, with the balance split among good bots and bad bots. The specific claim is defensible but varies by measurement.

claims.text: In 2013 the New York Times reported that half of YouTube traffic was bots masquerading as people, prompting internal fears of an 'inversion' event.

evidence.quote:
in 2013
the times reported that half of youtube
traffic was bots masquerading as people
and this was so scary that youtube
employees were worried about

claims.rationale: The source is the 2018 NY Mag 'The Inversion' essay by Max Read, which cited the earlier reporting; YouTube internal concern about bot traffic overtaking real traffic is documented.

claims.text: In a class-action suit, Facebook advertisers claim Facebook overestimates its traffic by between 150% and 900%.

evidence.quote:
uit filed by facebook
advertisers they claim that facebook
overestimates its traffic by between 150
and 900 percent now facebook claims they
only overstate their traffic by
60 to 8

claims.rationale: Class-action suits against Facebook over inflated potential-reach metrics are on the public record; the 150-900% figure comes from plaintiff pleadings. Facebook's own admission of 60-80% overstatement is also documented.

claims.text: Last year Facebook reportedly earned $84 billion in advertising revenue.

evidence.quote:
ers is that a lot
well last year facebook took in 84
billion dollars in ad revenue that's
actually grotesque
i agree facebook is not going t

claims.rationale: Meta's annual report for 2020 showed approximately $84 billion in advertising revenue — a figure consistent with the published 10-K. Video was recorded ~2021, so 'last year' aligns.

claims.text: The 'dead internet theory' — the idea that most online content is AI-generated — originated on 4chan, Wizardchan, and Agora Road, with a user named 'illuminati pirate' first naming it.

evidence.quote:
some of the darker corners of the web
places like 4chan wizardchan and agora
road the first person to put a name to
it goes by the online handle illuminati

claims.rationale: The 'Dead Internet Theory' was popularized by an Agora Road post by a user named IlluminatiPirate in January 2021 — confirmed by multiple reports including The Atlantic's coverage.

claims.text: Comedian Jordan Peele created a viral deepfake of President Obama as a demonstration of how convincing AI-generated political video can be.

someone else would now that video was a

what if in today's climate someone

claims.rationale: The April 2018 BuzzFeed/Monkeypaw 'You Won't Believe What Obama Says' deepfake by Jordan Peele is well documented and widely cited as an early viral deepfake demonstration.

claims.text: Instagram influencer 'Lil Miquela' (Miquela Sousa) with 3 million followers is a fully computer-generated fake persona.

claims.rationale: Lil Miquela is a confirmed CGI avatar operated by the LA-based agency Brud (founded 2016). The 3M-follower figure is in line with Instagram analytics from 2021.

claims.text: In 2018 it was revealed that Lil Miquela was not real, confessed by the media/marketing company Brud.

evidence.quote:
aled that she was
completely fake computer generated no no
her fans couldn't believe it eventually

brud confessed that she wasn't real d

claims.rationale: Brud publicly confirmed in 2018 via a leaked pitch deck and followup statements that Miquela is a CGI character; widely reported.

claims.text: The Kargu-2 (cargo-2) drone quadcopter by Turkish defense contractor STM uses machine learning to classify targets and operates in autonomous swarms.

evidence.quote:
re's a drone quadcopter called cargo
2 produced by a defense contractor stm
developed in turkey cargo 2 uses machine
learning to classify and ide

claims.rationale: STM's Kargu-2 loitering munition is a real weapons system; UN Panel of Experts report S/2021/229 on Libya specifically mentions Kargu-2 hunting targets in autonomous mode.

claims.text: According to the UN, the Kargu-2 drones are programmed to attack targets without requiring an operator — a true fire-forget-and-find capability.

evidence.quote:
g to the un
the drones are programmed to attack
targets without requiring an operator in
effect a true fire forget and find
capability they just analyze a bunch of
data a

claims.rationale: The UN report S/2021/229 ¶63 describes Kargu-2 as programmed to attack targets 'without requiring data connectivity between the operator and the munition: in effect, a true fire, forget and find capability.' The video quotes this almost verbatim.

claims.text: A Google whistleblower has alleged Google possesses algorithms that write other algorithms, pointing to Google's AutoML as an existing example.

evidence.quote:
s a google
whistleblower has said that google has
algorithms that can write other
algorithms google's auto ml0 does
exactly this what does google say well
google

claims.rationale: Google AutoML and Neural Architecture Search are real, documented systems in which neural networks search for neural network architectures — so the technical claim about 'algorithms writing algorithms' is true. The whistleblower framing is more speculative.

claims.text: If AI can run social media accounts, attract millions of followers, generate billions of dollars, influence elections, and drop bombs without human intervention, then the internet really is dead.

evidence.quote:
s it's definitely true look if
artificial intelligence can create
social media accounts attract millions
of followers generate billions of
dollars influence elections and drop
bomb

claims.rationale: The host's summary verdict: if each component is individually true (and the video argues each is), the dead-internet conclusion follows. This is a rhetorical conditional rather than a factual assertion.
